confidence_rationale: Foundational TICS review establishing the cerebellum as a forward/inverse internal-model substrate. Directly evidences the architectural pattern that SD-003-prereq presupposes: a structure that takes efference copy + current state and outputs predicted next sensory state, the exact computational role of E2_x in MECH-256. The MOSAIC framing (multiple paired models with responsibility signals) supports SD-029's stream-specific framing over a monolithic forward model. Confidence is moderated by the motor-to-interoceptive transfer required to map this onto the harm-stream comparator (SD-029) and by the paper's age -- subsequent work has refined and distributed the forward-model story beyond cerebellum.
failure_signatures: Cerebellar lesion does not eliminate sense of agency, only its motor-precision component -- this constrains how completely a cerebellar-style forward model can carry the full self-attribution role.

MOSAIC's responsibility-signal mechanism for selecting among multiple paired models is theoretically clean but empirically under-specified; if SD-003-prereq adopts a multi-stream architecture (one E2_x per reafferent stream), the routing/selection mechanism is the next architectural question and is not solved here.

Forward-model errors are used both for trajectory correction and for credit assignment in the paper's account. SD-029 uses the residual specifically for agency attribution; the paper does not separate these two roles and a clean separation may be an REE-specific design problem.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The cerebellum implements internal models of the motor apparatus -- both inverse models (computing the motor command for a desired trajectory) and forward models (predicting the sensory consequences of a motor command). Forward-model predictions are used to overcome feedback delays, support motor learning, and contribute to motor awareness. The MOSAIC framework proposes that the brain maintains multiple paired forward-inverse models, with a context-dependent responsibility signal selecting which pair is active.
mapping.ree_translation: The forward model role described here -- f(state_t, motor_command_t) -> predicted_state_{t+1} -- is structurally identical to E2_x in MECH-256: f(z_x_{t-1}, a_actual) -> predicted_z_x. SD-003-prereq is the architectural-substrate claim that REE must instantiate something with this computational role; this paper evidences that the brain instantiates exactly this structure for the motor case. The MOSAIC multi-paired-model proposal supports SD-029's framing of one comparator per reafferent stream rather than a monolithic E2.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper concerns motor forward models in cerebellum; the extension to interoceptive (z_harm_s) forward models in non-cerebellar substrates is by analogy. Subsequent work has distributed forward-model functions across cerebellum, parietal cortex, and prefrontal regions; a strict cerebellum-only reading would understate the architecture. The MOSAIC responsibility-signal mechanism is theoretically clean but its biological implementation remains contested.
mapping.source_context: TICS review covering primate and human ocular following, reaching adaptation, lesion data, and computational modelling. Three foundational authors (Wolpert, Miall, Kawato). Heavily cited; the canonical statement of the forward-model account for the motor system.

confidence_components.notes: Source quality high -- TICS, foundational authors, heavily cited. Mapping fidelity high for the forward-model architectural role itself. Transfer risk is the main caveat: motor forward models do not automatically generalise to interoceptive/harm-stream comparators, and the leap is the largest single inferential step in SD-029.
